body_txt: The name "Valentine", both when used as a given name or a surname, is derived from the Latin Valentinius , which itself means "one with strength or potential". However, in the modern day the word "Valentine" is much more associated with St. Valentine and his eponymous holiday , and thus carries connotations of romance and sexuality that it did not originally. Thus, it can be tempting for authors to give characters that are thematically related to sex, love or perhaps Fanservice the first or last name "Valentine", as a way of subtly conveying this information. Do note that this can also apply to foreign variations of the name (e.g. the Spanish / Italian "Valentino", the Slavic "Valentin", etc.) as well. If the author wants to make this connection especially on the nose, they may give this character a red or pink color scheme, a Heart Symbol motif, etc. Subtrope of Meaningful Name (though it can certainly be an Ironic Name if the character is connected to the darker sides of intimacy and sexuality, such as by being a Yandere ).
